behavior_summary: This script manages egg lifecycle/state and the potential spawning (hatching) of an NPC from an egg item.

U8P_SKELEGG::leaveFastArea: when the actor/egg leaves the designated fast-area trigger, it checks the egg's numeric id/state and, if a 0x64 offset/marker is present (Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64), clears that marker by subtracting 0x64 via Egg::setEggId.

U8P_SKELEGG::hatch: attempts to convert the egg item into an NPC. It inspects the egg state, performs a random chance check (urandom with range 0x0A, compared to <= 0x08), marks the egg state by adding 0x64, guards against double-spawning (Item::isNpc and an internal counter local6), and if allowed creates/configures an NPC (Npc::create, Item::orStatus, Item::getX/Y/Z, Item::destroy, Item::pop, Npc::setInCombat, Npc::setTarget, Npc::setAlignment).
notable_control_flow: U8P_SKELEGG::leaveFastArea: single conditional branch — check Egg::getEggId(this) >= 0x64 then Egg::setEggId(... - 0x64, this).

U8P_SKELEGG::hatch: multiple condition checks including egg-id-based branches (>=0x64 and !=0x01 inferred), a probabilistic gate urandom(0x0A) <= 0x08, and spawn-guard checks (Item::isNpc == 1 or local6 == 2 skip spawning).
U8P_SKELEGG::hatch marks state by adding 0x64 before spawning and increments a local counter (local6) when a spawn occurs to limit repeats.
open_questions: What semantic meaning does the 0x64 offset have (a temporary 'marked-for-U8P_SKELEGG::hatch' state, cooldown, or 'spawn-queued' flag)? — inferred but not definitive.
Exact threshold/meaning of the urandom(0x0A) <= 0x08 check: observed as a high-probability success gate (~9/10) but implementation details (inclusive/exclusive bounds) are inferred.
Precise purpose and lifecycle scope of local6 (counter versus mode flag) and whether it persists across process invocations or is ephemeral per U8P_SKELEGG::hatch attempt.
Whether U8P_SKELEGG::hatch is invoked automatically by the engine at a fixed event or requires an explicit trigger (metadata marks it as process type 0x0210 but trigger context is not explicit).

main_function.name: U8P_SKELEGG::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Clear the egg's 0x64 state marker when the egg (or relevant actor) leaves the fast-area trigger, restoring the egg id to its base value by subtracting 0x64 if present.
entry_conditions: Triggered on U8P_SKELEGG::leaveFastArea (actor left the function's trigger region / left the fast-area).
Operates on the implicit 'this' egg instance context; no explicit parameters.

function_map.name: U8P_SKELEGG::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: fast-area exit handler that clears a per-egg offset/marker (0x64)

function_map.name: U8P_SKELEGG::hatch
function_map.role: attempt to U8P_SKELEGG::hatch item into an NPC: state checks, probabilistic gating, spawn creation and configuration

function_map.name: Egg::setEggId
function_map.role: write egg numeric id/state (used to add/subtract 0x64)

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: randomness source used for U8P_SKELEGG::hatch chance

function_map.name: Item::* and Npc::* (Item::isNpc, Item::getFrame, Npc::create, Item::orStatus, Item::getX/Y/Z, Item::destroy, Item::pop, Npc::setInCombat, Npc::setTarget, Npc::setAlignment)
function_map.role: item/NPC utilities used during U8P_SKELEGG::hatch spawn and configuration

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg id/state to test for presence of the 0x64 marker before clearing it.
Write the egg id/state with 0x64 subtracted to clear the marker.
Read egg state to decide U8P_SKELEGG::hatch behavior and whether to add 0x64 marker.
Perform a probabilistic check gating the U8P_SKELEGG::hatch attempt (observed call urandom(0x0A) compared to <= 0x08).
Mark the egg state by adding 0x64 when U8P_SKELEGG::hatch progression occurs.
Guard: check if the item already represents an NPC to avoid double-spawn.
Retrieve item frame/type to pass to Npc::create for NPC instantiation.
Create the NPC object in memory using the item's frame.
Apply a status flag (observed 0x0080) to the created entity.
Read the egg/item X coordinate for NPC spawn placement.
Read the egg/item Y coordinate for NPC spawn placement.
Read the egg/item Z/level coordinate for NPC spawn placement.
Destroy the egg item after preparing the NPC for spawn.
Place (pop) the created NPC into the world at recorded coordinates.
Mark the spawned NPC as in combat immediately after spawning.
Set the NPC's target (observed constant 0x01, likely player).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script manages egg lifecycle/state and the potential spawning (hatching) of an NPC from an egg item.
